user_query: I'm a farmer from Hebei. I keep my harvest yields in CSV files and my nephew gave me a quick Python script, but it's messy and only reads one file. Please review and refactor scripts/analyze.py to make it reliable and to produce clear outputs. Use only the files in this workspace and the Internet as described, and run the refactored code to generate the outputs. Requirements: 1) Data loading by inspecting the input directory: - Do not hardcode file names. Discover and load all CSV files under input/ whose names start with "crop_yields_" and end with ".csv" (at least the two provided files). Add a column named source_file with the base filename for each row. - Normalize fields: trim whitespace in crop values and lowercase them; parse yield_kg as a number. - Read the configuration from input/config.json. Only keep rows where crop is in crops_of_interest. Sort the remaining rows by yield_kg in descending order and select the top_n rows. - Write the selected rows to output/top_yields.csv with columns in this order: date,crop,yield_kg,plot_id,source_file. 2) Internet fetch and HTML extraction (essential): - As a proxy for my co-op bulletin, add a step that downloads the IANA-managed "Example Domain" page under the example.com domain. Save the raw HTML to output/fetched/example.com.html. - Extract the text and the first <p> element’s text from that page and write them to output/webpage_metadata.json with the fields: domain (set to "example.com"), title, first_paragraph, saved_html_path (relative path you saved above). 3) Reporting and code review deliverables: - Create output/summary.md that includes: the number of CSV files loaded, total rows read, rows after filtering, and a small list of the top_n rows (crop and yield_kg). Also include the extracted webpage metadata fields (domain, title, first_paragraph) so I can see the network step worked. - Create output/review_notes.md with a brief code review: list the main issues you found in the original scripts/analyze.py (e.g., hardcoded paths, duplicated reads, string-based sorting of yields), and summarize the key changes you made during refactoring. Constraints and expectations: - Keep scripts runnable locally. Accept --input-dir, --config, and --output-dir CLI options (defaults can point to input/ and output/). The refactoring should remove hardcoded file names, avoid duplicating logic, and sort yields numerically. - Make the Internet step part of the normal run and fail clearly if the page cannot be fetched. For this task, actually fetch the page so the HTML and JSON are created. - Do not require any credentials or private services. Save all outputs exactly to the paths specified above. Inputs are provided under input/ and scripts/. Please produce the following artifacts after your refactoring and run: - output/top_yields.csv - output/fetched/example.com.html - output/webpage_metadata.json - output/summary.md - output/review_notes.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
